Replication of ‘Sick Body, Vigilant Mind’

Josh Tybur, Ben Jones, Lisa DeBruine

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE)
library(TOSTER) # for equivalence tests
library(afex) # for SPSS-style ANOVAs; also loads lme4 and lmerTest for LMEMs
library(tidyverse) # for data wrangling

Load and Process Data from Inquisit

data.vu <- list.files(path = "rawdata/VU", 
                       pattern = "dat$", recursive = TRUE,
                       full.names = TRUE) %>%
  lapply(read_tsv) %>%
  bind_rows() %>%
  select(subject:stimulusitem5) %>%
  mutate(site = "VU")
## Warning: Missing column names filled in: 'X12' [12], 'X13' [13],
## 'X14' [14], 'X15' [15], 'X16' [16], 'X17' [17], 'X18' [18], 'X19' [19],
## 'X20' [20], 'X21' [21]
data.gl <- list.files(path = "rawdata/Glasgow", 
                       pattern = "dat$", recursive = TRUE,
                       full.names = TRUE) %>%
  lapply(read_tsv) %>%
  bind_rows() %>%
  select(subject:stimulusitem5) %>%
  mutate(site = "GL")

# files 7 and 8 have character responses 'Ctrl-Q'

colspec <- cols(response = col_double(), .default = col_guess())

# exclude test runs
mi.excl <- c(9999, 999, 333, 90909, 9994, 999914, 
             999913, 999911, 99998, 999910, 99996, 99999)

data.mi <- list.files(path = "rawdata/Michigan", 
                       pattern = "dat$", recursive = TRUE,
                       full.names = TRUE) %>%
  lapply(read_tsv, col_type = colspec) %>%
  bind_rows() %>%
  filter(!(subject %in% mi.excl)) %>%
  select(subject:stimulusitem5) %>%
  mutate(site = "MI")
## Warning: 2 parsing failures.
## row      col expected   actual                                                                                                  file
##   1 response a double Ctrl+'Q' 'rawdata/Michigan/Replication Study Data/disfigured dot probe replication study_raw_computer 7.iqdat'
##   2 response a double Ctrl+'Q' 'rawdata/Michigan/Replication Study Data/disfigured dot probe replication study_raw_computer 7.iqdat'
## Warning: 2 parsing failures.
## row      col expected   actual                                                                                                  file
##   3 response a double Ctrl+'Q' 'rawdata/Michigan/Replication Study Data/disfigured dot probe replication study_raw_computer 8.iqdat'
##   4 response a double Ctrl+'Q' 'rawdata/Michigan/Replication Study Data/disfigured dot probe replication study_raw_computer 8.iqdat'
dat.raw <- bind_rows(data.vu, data.gl, data.mi)
trials <- dat.raw %>%
  mutate(
    stim_id = str_replace(stimulusitem3, ".bmp", ""),
    target = str_replace(stimulusitem5, ".bmp", "")
  ) %>%
  select(-(stimulusitem1:stimulusitem5)) %>%
  filter(blockcode != "practice") %>%
  separate(trialcode, c("real", "position", "trial_type", "block")) %>%
  separate(stim_id, c("w", "face_sex", "face_type", "face_n"), sep = c(1, 2,3), remove = F) %>%
  select(-real, -w, -face_n) %>%
  mutate( # circle = 18, square = 23
    correct = case_when(
      target == "circle" & response == 18 ~ 1,
      target == "square" & response == 23 ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ 0
    ),
    face_type = recode(face_type, "d" = "disfigured", "n" = "normal")
  ) %>%
  rename(rt = latency, user_id = subject)

Raters

# haven::read_sav("rawdata/VU/VU Dot Probe Qualtrics.sav") %>%
#   write_csv("rawdata/VU/Dot Probe Questionnaire.csv")

raters.vu <- read_csv("rawdata/VU/Dot Probe Questionnaire.csv") %>%
  mutate(site = "VU")

# haven::read_sav("rawdata/Glasgow/Glasgow Dot Probe Qualtrics.sav") %>%
#   write_csv("rawdata/Glasgow/Dot Probe Questionnaire.csv")

raters.gl <- read_csv("rawdata/Glasgow/Dot Probe Questionnaire.csv") %>%
  mutate(site = "GL")

# haven::read_sav("rawdata/Michigan/Dot Probe Questionnaire - MICHIGAN_September 30, 2019_09.17.sav") %>%
#   write_csv("rawdata/Michigan/Dot Probe Questionnaire.csv")

raters.mi <- read_csv("rawdata/Michigan/Dot Probe Questionnaire.csv")  %>%
  mutate(site = "MI")

Perceived Vulnerability to Disease

column name subscale question
PVD1 germ aversion It really bothers me when people sneeze without covering their mouths.
PVD2 perceived infectability If an illness is ‘going around’, I will get it.
PVD3R germ aversion I am comfortable sharing a water bottle with a friend.
PVD4 germ aversion I do not like to write with a pencil someone else has obviously chewed on.
PVD5R perceived infectability My past experiences make me believe I am not likely to get sick even when my friends are sick.
PVD6 perceived infectability I have a history of susceptibility to infectious disease.
PVD7 germ aversion I prefer to wash my hands pretty soon after shaking someone’s hand.
PVD8 perceived infectability In general, I am very susceptible to colds, flu, and other infectious diseases.
PVD9 germ aversion I dislike wearing used clothes because you do not know what the last person who wore it was like.
PVD10 perceived infectability I am more likely than the people around me to catch an infectious disease.
PVD11R germ aversion My hands do not feel dirty after touching money.
PVD12R perceived infectability I am unlikely to catch a cold, flu, or other illness, even if it is ‘going around’.
PVD13R germ aversion It does not make me anxious to be around sick people.
PVD14R perceived infectability My immune system protects me from most illnesses that other people get.
PVD15 germ aversion I avoid using public telephones because of the risk that I may catch something from the previous user. 
raters <- bind_rows(raters.vu, raters.gl, raters.mi) %>%
  # reverse code items
  mutate(
    PVD3 = 8 - PVD3R,
    PVD5 = 8 - PVD5R,
    PVD11 = 8 - PVD11R,
    PVD12 = 8 - PVD12R,
    PVD13 = 8 - PVD13R,
    PVD14 = 8 - PVD14R,
    sex = NA_character_,
    age = NA_real_
  ) %>%
  select(
    site, sex, age, 
    user_id = PID,
    recent_cold = DH1,
    recency_1 = DH2,
    recency_2 = DH3,
    recency_3 = DH4,
    recency_4 = DH5,
    PVDpi_1 = PVD2,
    PVDpi_2 = PVD5,
    PVDpi_3 = PVD6,
    PVDpi_4 = PVD8,
    PVDpi_5 = PVD10,
    PVDpi_6 = PVD12,
    PVDpi_7 = PVD14,
    PVDga_1 = PVD1,
    PVDga_2 = PVD3,
    PVDga_3 = PVD4,
    PVDga_4 = PVD7,
    PVDga_5 = PVD9,
    PVDga_6 = PVD11,
    PVDga_7 = PVD13,
    PVDga_8 = PVD15,
    pd_1 = DS1,
    pd_2 = DS2,
    pd_3 = DS3,
    pd_4 = DS4,
    pd_5 = DS5,
    pd_6 = DS6,
    pd_7 = DS7,
    img_1 = A1b___yellow_stuff,
    img_2 = A2b_sickface,
    img_3 = A3b_Crowded_train,
    img_4 = A4b_Yellow_red_towel,
    img_5 = A5b_wound,
    img_6 = A6b_worms,
    img_7 = A7b_mite
  )

Recode

raters_coded <- raters %>%
  mutate(
    recent_cold = case_when(
      recent_cold %in% 1:3 ~ "recent",
      recent_cold %in% 4:7 ~ "not recent"
    )
  ) %>%
  gather(var, score, recency_1:img_7) %>%
  separate(var, c("var", "var_n")) %>%
  group_by(user_id, sex, age, recent_cold, var) %>%
  summarise(score = mean(score)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  spread(var, score)

Exclusions

“Trials with incorrect categorization responses and trials with latencies greater than 3 standard deviations above or below a participant’s mean latency were excluded. Two participants had high error rates (> 3 SD above the mean) and were excluded.” (Miller & Maner)

trials_inc <- trials %>%
  filter(correct == 1, trial_type == "diff") %>%
  group_by(user_id) %>%
  mutate(mean_rt = mean(rt),
         sd_rt = sd(rt),
         n_correct = n()) %>%
  filter(rt <= mean_rt + 3*sd_rt, rt >= mean_rt - 3*sd_rt) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by() %>%
  filter(n_correct >= mean(n_correct) - 3*sd(n_correct)) %>%
  ungroup()

Join Data

# centre and scale continuous scores from only included raters
raters_inc <- raters_coded %>%
  semi_join(trials_inc, by = "user_id") %>%
  mutate(
    recent_cold = as.factor(recent_cold),
    recency.z = (recency - mean(recency)) / sd(recency),
    PVDga.z = (PVDga - mean(PVDga)) / sd(PVDga),
    PVDpi.z = (PVDpi - mean(PVDpi)) / sd(PVDpi)
  )

data_agg <- trials_inc %>%
  group_by(user_id, face_type) %>%
  summarise(rt = mean(rt)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  left_join(raters_inc, by = "user_id") %>%
  filter(!is.na(recent_cold))

write_csv(data_agg, "processed/SBVM_data_aggregated.csv")

Descriptives

Demographics

raters_coded %>%
  group_by(sex) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_age = mean(age, na.rm = T),
    sd_age = sd(age, na.rm = T)
  ) %>%
  bind_rows(raters_coded %>%
    group_by() %>%
    summarise(
      n = n(),
      mean_age = mean(age, na.rm = T),
      sd_age = sd(age, na.rm = T)
    ) %>%
    mutate(sex = "TOTAL")
  ) %>%
  mutate_if(is.numeric, round, 2)

Included trials

trials_inc %>%
  group_by(user_id, face_type) %>%
  summarise(included_trials = n()) %>%
  group_by(face_type) %>%
  summarise(n = n(),
            mean = mean(included_trials),
            min = min(included_trials),
            max = max(included_trials)
  ) %>%
  mutate_if(is.numeric, round, 2)

RTs by trial

trials_inc %>%
  ggplot(aes(rt, color = face_type, group = stim_id)) +
  geom_density() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#CC0000", "#006699"), name = "Face Type") +
  theme_minimal()

Illness Variables

raters_coded %>%
  gather(variable, score, recency, PVDga, PVDpi) %>%
  ggplot(aes(score, color = variable)) +
  geom_density()

Face Type by Recent Cold

data_agg %>%
  group_by(face_type, recent_cold) %>%
  summarise(n = n(),
            mean = mean(rt),
            sd = sd(rt)
  ) %>%
  mutate_if(is.numeric, round, 3)
ggplot(data_agg, aes(recent_cold, rt, color = face_type)) +
  geom_violin(aes(fill = face_type), alpha = 0.25) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.2, outlier.size = 0,
               position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
               show.legend = F) +
  labs(x = "Recency of Cold",
       y = "Mean RT on Attentional Shift Trials") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#CC0000", "#006699"), name = "Face Type") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#CC0000", "#006699"), guide = F) +
  theme_minimal()

Analyses

Reaction Time DV

Analysis 1A: Categorical illness recency

cat_ANOVA <- aov_4(rt ~ recent_cold * face_type + 
                     (1 + face_type | user_id), 
              data = data_agg, factorize = FALSE)

anova(cat_ANOVA, es = "pes", MSE = FALSE)

Analysis 1B: Recent illness

recent_ill <- filter(data_agg, recent_cold == "recent")
t.test(recent_ill$rt~recent_ill$face_type)
## 
##  Welch Two Sample t-test
## 
## data:  recent_ill$rt by recent_ill$face_type
## t = 0.69158, df = 295.33, p-value = 0.4897
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -28.99479  60.41342
## sample estimates:
## mean in group disfigured     mean in group normal 
##                 669.3399                 653.6305

Analysis 1C: Non-Recent illness

not_recent_ill <- filter(data_agg, recent_cold == "not recent")
t.test(not_recent_ill$rt~not_recent_ill$face_type)
## 
##  Welch Two Sample t-test
## 
## data:  not_recent_ill$rt by not_recent_ill$face_type
## t = 0.54347, df = 496.26, p-value = 0.587
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -19.47714  34.37254
## sample estimates:
## mean in group disfigured     mean in group normal 
##                 629.9990                 622.5513

Analysis 1D: Equivalence Tests

stats_cat <- data_agg %>%
  spread(face_type, rt, convert = T) %>%
  mutate(dv = disfigured - normal) %>%
  group_by(recent_cold) %>%
  summarise(n = n(),
            m = mean(dv),
            sd = sd(dv)
  )

n1 <- stats_cat %>% filter(recent_cold == "not recent") %>% pull(n)
n2 <- stats_cat %>% filter(recent_cold == "recent") %>% pull(n)
m1 <- stats_cat %>% filter(recent_cold == "not recent") %>% pull(m)
m2 <- stats_cat %>% filter(recent_cold == "recent") %>% pull(m)
sd1 <- stats_cat %>% filter(recent_cold == "not recent") %>% pull(sd)
sd2 <- stats_cat %>% filter(recent_cold == "recent") %>% pull(sd)
low_eqbound_dz <- -.35
high_eqbound_dz <- .35

TOSTtwo(m1, m2, sd1, sd2, n1, n2, low_eqbound_dz, high_eqbound_dz)

## TOST results:
## t-value lower bound: 2.00    p-value lower bound: 0.023
## t-value upper bound: -4.56   p-value upper bound: 0.000004
## degrees of freedom : 249.04
## 
## Equivalence bounds (Cohen's d):
## low eqbound: -0.35 
## high eqbound: 0.35
## 
## Equivalence bounds (raw scores):
## low eqbound: -21.2097 
## high eqbound: 21.2097
## 
## TOST confidence interval:
## lower bound 90% CI: -18.94
## upper bound 90% CI:  2.417
## 
## NHST confidence interval:
## lower bound 95% CI: -21
## upper bound 95% CI:  4.477
## 
## Equivalence Test Result:
## The equivalence test was significant, t(249.04) = 2.002, p = 0.0232, given equivalence bounds of -21.210 and 21.210 (on a raw scale) and an alpha of 0.05.
## Null Hypothesis Test Result:
## The null hypothesis test was non-significant, t(249.04) = -1.277, p = 0.203, given an alpha of 0.05.
## Based on the equivalence test and the null-hypothesis test combined, we can conclude that the observed effect is statistically not different from zero and statistically equivalent to zero.

Continuous illness recency

cont_ANOVA <- aov_4(rt ~ recency.z * face_type + 
                      (1 + face_type | user_id), 
              data = data_agg, factorize = FALSE)

anova(cont_ANOVA, es = "pes", MSE = FALSE)

Self-report Measures

Difference in PVD between recently ill and non-recently ill groups

Analysis 2A: Perceived Infectability

t.test(data_agg$PVDpi~data_agg$recent_cold)
## 
##  Welch Two Sample t-test
## 
## data:  data_agg$PVDpi by data_agg$recent_cold
## t = -3.8382, df = 642.45, p-value = 0.0001362
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.2036337 -0.0657930
## sample estimates:
## mean in group not recent     mean in group recent 
##                 3.344000                 3.478713

Analysis 2B: Germ Aversion

t.test(data_agg$PVDga~data_agg$recent_cold)
## 
##  Welch Two Sample t-test
## 
## data:  data_agg$PVDga by data_agg$recent_cold
## t = -3.078, df = 619.25, p-value = 0.002176
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.23072222 -0.05098639
## sample estimates:
## mean in group not recent     mean in group recent 
##                 3.956000                 4.096854

Analysis 2C: TDDS

t.test(data_agg$pd~data_agg$recent_cold)
## 
##  Welch Two Sample t-test
## 
## data:  data_agg$pd by data_agg$recent_cold
## t = -2.9281, df = 603.23, p-value = 0.003539
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.34203042 -0.06741518
## sample estimates:
## mean in group not recent     mean in group recent 
##                 4.915429                 5.120151

Analysis 2D: Curtis Images

t.test(data_agg$img~data_agg$recent_cold)
## 
##  Welch Two Sample t-test
## 
## data:  data_agg$img by data_agg$recent_cold
## t = -1.364, df = 618.55, p-value = 0.1731
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.28386207  0.05116576
## sample estimates:
## mean in group not recent     mean in group recent 
##                 4.288571                 4.404920
